parsed_review.summary: CP00-356 is a descriptor-only Risk-A closeout pack (10 units, RP11.P07.M03.S19-RP11.P07.M04.S06) covering the M03 Primary Implementation Slice failure tail (audit failure hint, Hermes failure evidence, Claude edge-case prompt, human escalation note) and the M04 Secondary Workflow Slice head (failure taxonomy, missing tenant/actor/Matter/resource, unknown action failures). The pack-level production_ready claim is correctly FALSE (implementation_layer=descriptor, runtime_ready=false); the production_ready_flag 'time_expense_core_failure_slice_descriptor_verified' is supported and not over-claimed. Verifications: (1) Implementation complete and self-consistent — all 7 required public exports are defined (createTimeExpenseCoreCp356FailureSliceDescriptor/CaseSet in service.js; validateTimeExpenseCoreCp356Coverage/FailureSliceDescriptor and the Hermes/Claude packet + handoff builders in validators.js), surfaced through index.js export *, with the row-key helper mapping all 10 titles and inherited CP355 ROW_EXTRAS supplying every field the descriptor validator asserts (audit_hint_detail_included=false, expected_outcome=rejected_customer_safe, etc.). (2) Cross-artifact agreement — the live closeout-pack-plan.json CP00-356 entry (incl. included_units/deliverable_type), the contract current_pack/cp356_requirements/cp356_no_write_attestation and historical_pack_bindings tail, the registry binding, and the manifest all agree on unit_count=10, range, and the deliverable distribution {security_audit:1,hermes_evidence:1,claude_review:1,implementation:1,failure_recovery:6}; the validator script deep-equals contract against registry constants. (3) Authority boundary preserved — claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, enterprise trust not claimed from local validation; review request is read-only with Read/Grep/Glob only. (4) Hermes used only for deterministic review packet/receipt — emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, receipt_shape descriptor_only..._no_write, no product runtime dispatch. (5) No-write/no-real-data boundaries broadly attested (synthetic_only, no_real_data, no DB/object-storage/audit writes) and consistent with the pure descriptor-builder code read. (6) Review hardening sound — readiness auth preflight passed with valid JSON, prompt within size limit and excludes full diff/source, no auth failure / 0-byte / malformed-JSON / tool-call-shaped evidence is being passed off as valid, and valid_review_receipts/invalid_review_attempts are empty with status pending. Tests (model.test.js) cover coverage validity, descriptor-only rows, authority boundaries, evidence packets, and the CP00-357 handoff. The three downstream artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) and the adjudication/inspection steps are legitimately not yet present at this read_only_claude_run_raw_capture stage of the hardening sequence — they consume this review — so their absence is expected, not a defect. As a read-only reviewer I could not execute the listed validation commands; the command-evidence claims all exit 0, and every static artifact I inspected is fully consistent with those claims. No P0/P1/P2 issues found; two informational P3 notes recorded.

findings.title: Downstream review/adjudication/inspection artifacts not yet present (expected pre-review state)
findings.details: manifest.evidence_refs and required_pack_artifacts list docs/closeout-packs/cp00-356/claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, but only manifest.json and command-evidence.json currently exist in that directory. This is consistent with pack_level_claude_review.status='pending', empty valid_review_receipts/invalid_review_attempts, and the hardening_sequence in which finding_adjudication and construction_inspection run AFTER read_only_claude_run_raw_capture (this review). The three artifacts are produced by steps that consume this review, so their absence at this stage is correct, not a missing-evidence defect.
findings.recommendation: No action for this review. Ensure the downstream normalize/receipt/adjudication/inspection steps generate these three artifacts before final closeout and commit, as command-evidence already gates the post-review commands on claude-review-result.json.

findings.title: required_no_leak_guards narrowed vs CP00-355 (intentional scope, result-level guards retained)
findings.details: CP00-356 required_no_leak_guards lists 6 guards, dropping lock_token_excluded and fixture_payload_excluded that appeared in CP00-355's 8-guard list. This matches the narrower CP00-356 row scope (the M03 failure tail and M04 head contain no lock-conflict or failure-fixture rows). The result-level attestation in service.js freezeCp356Result still sets exposes_lock_token=false and exposes_fixture_payload=false, and the manifest implementation_summary still asserts no_lock_token_leak=true and no_fixture_payload_leak=true, so there is no actual leak-protection regression — only the descriptor's declared required-guard set is scoped to the rows present.
findings.recommendation: No action; the scoping is correct for this pack. If a downstream pack reintroduces lock-conflict or fixture rows, restore lock_token_excluded and fixture_payload_excluded to that pack's required_no_leak_guards.
